mnemonics: Stars and Bars: Imagine placing stars (*) for items and bars (|) to separate categories. The formula is C(n + k - 1, k), which you can remember as 'Choose (n plus k minus 1) take k'.
keyPoints: Combinations with repetition allow for the selection of k items from n types, where repetition is allowed and order does not matter.
The standard formula is C(n + k - 1, k) or equivalently C(n + k - 1, n - 1).
This problem is equivalent to finding the number of non-negative integer solutions to the equation x₁ + x₂ + ... + xₙ = k.
commonMistakes: Confusing combinations with repetition with standard combinations (without repetition) and using the formula C(n, k) instead.
Forgetting that the formula is for non-negative integers and misapplying it to problems with minimum requirements (e.g., at least one of each type) without adjusting the variables.
